question_title: Will the community prediction be higher than 36.00% on 2026-04-22 for the Metaculus question "Will another U.S. cabinet member leave office before May 2026?"?
summary: Meta-prediction on whether Metaculus CP for cabinet departure question exceeds 36% on April 22. CP is currently exactly at 36.00% - right at the knife's edge. The turbulent political environment (Iran conflict, two recent cabinet firings, active speculation about Hegseth and Gabbard) provides a mild tilt toward CP moving above threshold. There's also a ~15-20% chance of actual cabinet departure resolving this YES directly. Status quo persistence and White House denials partially offset. Overall probability of ~58% YES.

factors.description: CP currently exactly at threshold (36.00%) - needs only a tiny upward movement to resolve YES, but staying flat or moving down resolves NO
Active media speculation about multiple cabinet members (Hegseth, Gabbard) being next to be fired; NBC reports Trump 'considering more changes in coming weeks'
Turbulent political environment: Iran conflict creating internal cabinet friction, two firings in quick succession showing Trump's willingness to act
Gabbard narrowly avoided firing (Roger Stone intervention) - she remains vulnerable; Hegseth facing scapegoat narrative for Iran situation
If underlying event actually occurs (cabinet member leaves by Apr 22), meta-question resolves YES directly - estimated ~15-20% chance in next 8 days
Status quo persistence: White House denied firing rumors, Trump reportedly wants to avoid 'massive shake-up', most speculation doesn't materialize
CP could drift down as time passes without a firing - approaching deadline reduces underlying probability
85 forecasters provide moderate stability but not extreme inertia - CP can move with news

reasoning:
The reflection was approved. Let me finalize my forecast.

**Summary of analysis:**

This is a meta-prediction about whether the Metaculus CP on "Will another U.S. cabinet member leave office before May 2026?" will be above 36.00% on April 22. The CP is currently exactly at the threshold (36.00% as of April 13).

**Key considerations:**

1. **Knife-edge threshold**: CP needs only a tiny upward move to resolve YES, but flat/down resolves NO
2. **Volatile political environment**: Iran conflict, two recent firings (Noem, Bondi), active speculation about Hegseth and Gabbard
3. **Concrete signals**: NBC reports Trump "considering more changes in coming weeks"; experts predict Hegseth firing; Gabbard narrowly survived
4. **Direct resolution path**: ~15-20% chance a cabinet member actually departs before April 22, which resolves this YES automatically
5. **Counterbalance**: White House denied rumors; Trump reportedly wants to avoid "massive shake-up"; status quo persistence

**Decomposition**: P(YES) ≈ P(departure by Apr 22) + P(no departure) × P(CP > 36% | no departure) ≈ 0.18 + 0.82 × 0.50 ≈ 0.59

resolution_criteria: This question will resolve based on the community prediction of the Metaculus question found [here](https://www.metaculus.com/questions/43099) (i.e. the 'target question'). If the community prediction of the target question on 2026-04-22 11:47:50 is higher than 36.00%, this question will resolve to 'Yes'. If it is lower or equal to 36.00%, this question will resolve to 'No'. If the target question has already resolved before this question opens, then this question will be annulled. If the target question resolves after this question opens, but before 2026-04-22 11:47:50, then this question will resolve to the same value as the target question.
